skill.name: maxhub-temp-mail
skill.description: Temporary email service assistant. Create disposable mailboxes, list received emails, and read message details via the MaxHub API.
skill.summary: Generate temporary email addresses and retrieve their messages through the MaxHub API.
skill.author: XieWxx

security_audit.summary: After evaluating all 75 static findings, no confirmed security threats were found. The skill is a legitimate API client for the MaxHub temp-mail service (www.aconfig.cn). All external command executions are curl calls targeting documented API endpoints. The environment variable MAXHUB_API_KEY is the skill's own declared credential, used only for API authentication. The heuristic critical finding (code execution + network + credential access) is a false positive: this combination is expected and necessary for an API client skill to function. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' and 'system reconnaissance' findings are false positives caused by pipe characters in markdown tables and feature list descriptions respectively. The skill includes proper credential handling instructions and does not exfiltrate data.

medium_findings.title: Shell curl commands for API calls
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the AI to execute curl commands to call API endpoints. This is expected behavior for an API client skill, but users should be aware the AI will run external commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill contains curl command templates in documentation blocks. These are legitimate API calls but do require shell execution. The commands use only documented, hardcoded endpoints with environment variable substitution, no user-controlled injection points.

medium_findings.title: Environment variable used for API authentication
medium_findings.description: The skill reads MAXHUB_API_KEY from the environment for API authentication. This is declared in the skill metadata and is the intended authentication mechanism, not a credential theft attempt.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The env access is fully documented in metadata as a required dependency. The skill also includes credential safety instructions (line 159: 'Keep API key values out of output'). No evidence of exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs to external API service
low_findings.description: Multiple hardcoded URLs point to www.aconfig.cn, which is the legitimate MaxHub API service. These are documentation references and API endpoint URLs, not malicious redirects.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs consistently reference the same domain (www.aconfig.cn) which is the declared API provider. No external or unknown domains are referenced. The URLs are used in documentation and curl command examples only.

low_findings.title: Markdown table pipes misidentified as weak cryptography
low_findings.description: The static analyzer detected pipe characters (|) in markdown table formatting as 'weak cryptographic algorithm' patterns. These are standard markdown table delimiters, not hash or encryption artifacts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The patterns are clearly markdown table syntax, not cryptographic material. No actual encryption, hashing, or encoding operations exist in this skill.

low_findings.title: Feature descriptions misidentified as system reconnaissance
low_findings.description: API endpoint feature names like 'Email Operations' and API function names like 'get_temp_email' were flagged as system reconnaissance. These are standard API documentation terms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are simple feature descriptions in README files and routing tables. No actual system enumeration commands or reconnaissance behavior is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: API key used in curl Authorization header
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses MAXHUB_API_KEY as a Bearer token in curl commands. This is the standard and expected authentication pattern for HTTP API clients. The skill explicitly instructs keeping key values out of output (SKILL.md line 159).
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is a standard API client authentication flow. The credential is an env variable, not hardcoded. The skill includes credential safety guidance. No exfiltration or misuse documented.

content.user_title: Create temporary emails and read messages instantly
content.value_statement: Users need temporary email addresses to protect their privacy when signing up for online services. This skill creates disposable mailboxes, retrieves received messages, and reads email content through the MaxHub API without exposing personal data.
actual_capabilities: Generate a new temporary email address on demand
Retrieve the list of received emails for any active temp mailbox
Read full email content including sender, subject, and body text
Detect user language and respond in Chinese or English accordingly
Execute autonomous API calls using curl with the configured API key
Handle error responses gracefully with retry logic for transient failures
limitations: Requires a valid MAXHUB_API_KEY obtained from www.aconfig.cn
Cannot send emails, only receive them to temporary addresses
Email lifespan and storage duration depend on the MaxHub service limits
API rate limits of 429 may apply with high request frequency

use_cases.title: Privacy Protection Registration
use_cases.description: Generate a temporary email address to sign up for online services without exposing your personal email inbox to spam or marketing messages.

use_cases.title: Automated Testing Verification
use_cases.description: Create disposable mailboxes during automated test suites that require email verification steps such as account registration confirmation with one-time codes.

use_cases.title: One-Time Signup Inboxes
use_cases.description: Use a temporary inbox for one-off website registrations or service trials to prevent long-term spam and marketing emails in your primary account.
use_cases.target_user: General users who value inbox cleanliness

prompt_templates.title: Create a new temporary email
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a temporary email address for me to use.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs a fresh disposable email address for a one-time registration

prompt_templates.title: Check inbox for new messages
prompt_templates.prompt: Check if I have received any new emails in my temporary inbox.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to see incoming messages after sharing their temp email address

prompt_templates.title: Read a specific email
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the contents of the most recent email in my inbox and tell me what it says.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to view the full text of a received message such as a verification code

prompt_templates.title: Automated verification code extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a temporary email, then wait for incoming messages and extract any verification codes you find. Summarize the results.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer running automated tests that need to capture and use email verification codes

output_examples.input: Create a temporary email address for me.
output_examples.output: I have created a temporary email address: user-8k3m2@temp.maxhub.com. You can use this address to register for services. Ask me to check for new messages anytime.

output_examples.input: Do I have any new emails?
output_examples.output:
You have 2 new messages in your inbox:
1. Welcome to Our Service - from welcome@example.com (received 2 minutes ago)
2. Your Verification Code: 729184 - from verify@service.com (received just now)

best_practices: Verify that MAXHUB_API_KEY is set in the environment before making any API calls to avoid authorization failures.
Handle 429 rate limit errors by waiting before retrying and 502 server errors with an automatic single retry.
Keep the API key value out of all displayed output to prevent accidental credential exposure.
anti_patterns: Hardcoding the API key value directly in curl commands instead of using the MAXHUB_API_KEY environment variable.
Sending duplicate API calls for the same data without checking for cached results in the cache_url response field.
Mixing Chinese and English text in the same response instead of matching the user detected language consistently.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a valid MAXHUB_API_KEY from www.aconfig.cn. Sign up on the website and create an API key in your dashboard.

faq.question: Can this skill send emails?
faq.answer: No, the skill only supports receiving emails to temporary addresses. It cannot send emails from the temp mailbox.

faq.question: How long does a temporary email address last?
faq.answer: The lifespan depends on the MaxHub service. Check www.aconfig.cn for current limits on email storage duration.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill only communicates with the official MaxHub API at www.aconfig.cn using your own API key. No data is sent to unknown or unauthorized services.

faq.question: Can I use this skill in Chinese?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill detects your language automatically and responds in Chinese or English. Numbers are also formatted for your locale.

faq.question: What happens if I exceed the API rate limit?
faq.answer: The skill will report a 429 error. You should wait a moment before making more requests. The skill does not automatically retry on rate limits.
